title: Moulton allegedly knocks phone from questioner’s hand after being asked about Graham Platner
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rep. Seth Moulton allegedly knocked a phone from a questioner's hand during a Massachusetts event, sparking controversy.
Moulton's campaign claimed the person was a 'political tracker' from America Rising, not a Fox News reporter.
Fox News denied paying the individual, clarifying the person was not their reporter after Moulton's campaign intervention.

title: Durbin homers to break tie in the Red Sox’s 6-3 win over the Yankees
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Caleb Durbin hit a two-run homer in the fifth inning, leading the Boston Red Sox to a 6-3 win over the Yankees.
Connelly Early allowed two runs over six innings and struck out nine; Aroldis Chapman earned his 15th save for the Red Sox.
Yankees starter Cam Schlittler gave up four unearned runs and struck out nine, with New York committing four errors.

title: 3 injured when car crashes into Newburyport restaurant
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A black BMW X3 crashed into Newburyport's Black Cow restaurant, injuring three customers on Thursday evening.
The 67-year-old female driver from Groveland was uninjured; alcohol is not suspected as a factor, police stated.
No structural damage to the building was found, but the bar area requires repairs; the restaurant remains open.

title: Suspect in custody after man, 58, fatally stabbed in Wellesley park
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 58-year-old man was fatally stabbed Thursday night at Phillips Park in Wellesley, Massachusetts.
James Floyd Bennett III, 26, was arrested and is expected to be arraigned Friday in Dedham District Court.
Authorities say there is no threat to public safety as the investigation by State and Wellesley police continues.

title: Framingham police will not renew Flock Safety contract after months of resident opposition
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Framingham Police will not renew the Flock Safety contract, ending use of license plate reader cameras after resident protests.
The decision follows residents' concerns over privacy, data sharing, and potential inappropriate use by federal authorities.
Framingham joins other Massachusetts cities like Cambridge and Natick in discontinuing contracts with Flock Safety.

title: ‘Over a foot of manure’: Horses, mules among animals rescued from Western Mass. property, MSPCA says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MSPCA-Angell rescued 13 animals from a Western Massachusetts farm due to unsanitary conditions, including a foot of manure.
Animals rescued include seven horses, two mules, a donkey, two geese, and a cockatoo; some were underweight with overgrown hooves.
Rescued animals are currently at MSPCA-Angell at Nevins Farm receiving care and will be available for adoption.

title: 10 players Bruins could target in 1st round of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins hold the 23rd overall pick in the 2026 NHL Draft after losing Toronto's top pick.
Key prospects for the Bruins include defenseman Tommy Bleyl and forwards J.P. Hurlbert and Nikita Klepov.
The Bruins possess eight picks in the draft, spanning from the first to the seventh round.

title: Coyote warning issued in Beverly after dog attacked on trail
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Beverly police issued a coyote warning after a dog was attacked near the Fox Hill Trail entrance on Tall Tree Drive.
A coyote with pups was found in a den 30 feet from the trail entrance; the dog suffered unspecified injuries.
Residents are urged to keep dogs leashed and report aggressive coyote behavior as it's their denning season.

title: Preserving a vehicle’s computer settings while replacing a battery
author: John Paul
bullets: AAA Northeast's Car Doctor advises using a jump pack to maintain computer memory during battery replacement.
Elevated idle speeds in the 2026 Subaru Forester during start are normal and not harmful when driving off.
For a 2011 Ram 1500, quality aftermarket brake pads offer cost-effective alternatives to original equipment parts.

title: Nationwide rental listings are most affordable in years, Zillow says. In Boston? Not quite.
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Zillow report shows 74% of national rental listings are affordable for median-income families; Boston's rate is only 50%.
Boston's typical rent is $3,211, the fourth-highest in the USA, with few listings under $1,000 monthly.
Boston's rent increased by 2.5%, with housing developments converting offices to homes to tackle housing issues.
